title: Packed checkpoint storage: key + length + value in single slot with sentinel address escape hatch
trigger_patterns: checkpoint storage with timestamp and value
packed struct in single storage slot
value too large for packed slot
explanation: Instead of two slots per checkpoint (one for key, one for value), solady packs key(48 bits) + array length(48 bits) + value(160 bits) into a single uint256. For values > 160 bits, it uses address() as a sentinel marker in the packed slot and stores the full value at not(checkpointSlot) — the bitwise NOT of the slot address, which is a deterministic overflow location that doesn't collide with any keccak256 derived slot.
gas_saved_unit: per checkpoint write (saves 1 SSTORE for most values)
when_to_apply: Append-only checkpoint arrays where key fits in 48 bits and value usually fits in 160 bits
when_not_to_apply: Mutable arrays, checkpoints where values regularly exceed 160 bits, any pattern requiring random access writes
